requirement: Buyer can initiate the supplier onboarding process by providing basic supplier information.
Internal approval workflow is triggered after buyer initiates the supplier onboarding process.
Supplier receives an invitation via email to register with AECI and complete due diligence steps after internal approval.
Supplier information is accurate and current.
Visibility to certifications and contact information is provided.
Multiple channels of managing information are eliminated.
Supplier status can be managed, including purchase block, payment block, supplier deactivation, and reactivation.
Approval workflows involving Procurement, Finance, General Accounting, and Master Data teams are implemented.
Integration touchpoints between Coupa SIM and SAP for supplier creation, updates, and activation are established.
Internal users can register new suppliers in Coupa for urgent or business-critical situations, adhering to governance and approval controls.
Internal users can update existing supplier information in Coupa, including status changes.
Internal users can extend supplier contracts in Coupa.
Internal users can create one-time vendors in Coupa with a simplified approval flow and reduced data requirements.
Users can view supplier portal status in the Suppliers table.
The supplier onboarding process will be standardized across geographies, with exceptions for Australia and other countries.
Field-level requirements for supplier onboarding will vary by geography, based on local guidelines and legal requirements.
Coupa will support auto-translation of standard information on the user interface for multiple languages.
Process owners and document reviewers can acknowledge and sign the document.
